title: Access Ocean Tracking Network Data
text: otndata wrapper around Ocean Tracking Network’s Plone content management system setup.

title: Access Ocean Tracking Network Data
text: can install development version otndata GitHub :

title: Access Ocean Tracking Network Data
text: basic example shows solve common problem: things can accessed without logging . Mainly information presented via leaflet map can seen https://members.oceantrack.org. can also query projects according code, country origin, species, institution, node, point contact. ’ll likely need log access parts CMS. can set username password system using otn_set_credentials helper function. , can just log using otn_login. package meant interface node’s Plone instance. can switch using server argument. List project’s files: , just grab ones modified recently using since argument:
code: library(otndata) otn_list_species() |> head() #> scientificname commonname #> 1 Abramis brama common bream #> 2 Acanthocybium solandri wahoo #> 3 Acanthurus bahianus cirujano pardo #> 4 Acanthurus blochii ringtail surgeonfish #> 5 Acanthurus chirurgus doctorfish #> 6 Acanthurus coeruleus blue tang otn_list_projects() |> head() #> node collectioncode country longitude latitude #> 1 ETN Usk:2021-2024 UNITED KINGDOM -4.035 52.405 #> 2 ETN V2LTROT NORWAY 10.945 63.605 #> 3 ETN OTN-Tosenfjorden NORWAY 6.140 68.715 #> 4 ETN ST08SWE SWEDEN 10.960 58.465 #> 5 OTN RNP NORWAY 10.385 63.420 #> 6 OTN V2LBFC MEXICO, BELIZE -87.650 18.625 #> shortname #> 1 River Usk receiver network 2021-2024 #> 2 OTN-Loan_Salmonids in Trondheimsfjorden #> 3 OTN-Tosenfjorden,OTN-Tosenfjorden #> 4 sea trout_2018-9_gullmarn,sea trout_2018-9_gullmarn #> 5 NTNU River Nidelva Project #> 6 Big Fish Caribbean #> longname #> 1 River Usk receiver network 2021-2024 #> 2 OTN_Loan_Salmonids in Trondheimsfjorden #> 3 Marine migrations and area use of brown trout in Tosenfjorden, Bindal municipality 2015-2017 #> 4 Sea trout Salmo trutta connectivity in Gullmarn fjord, Skagerrak #> 5 Migration of anadromous brown trout between river and fjord, Nidelva, Norway #> 6 Big Fish: Cooperative monitoring network for fish spawning aggregations in the wider Caribbean #> ocean website datacenter_infourl #> 1 NE ATLANTIC http://www.lifewatch.be/en/imis?dasid=6491&show=json http://www.lifewatch.be/etn/ #> 2 NE ATLANTIC http://www.lifewatch.be/en/imis?dasid=8311&show=json http://www.lifewatch.be/etn/ #> 3 NE ATLANTIC http://www.lifewatch.be/en/imis?dasid=6201&show=json http://www.lifewatch.be/etn/ #> 4 NE ATLANTIC http://www.lifewatch.be/en/imis?dasid=8137&show=json http://www.lifewatch.be/etn/ #> 5 NE ATLANTIC https://members.oceantrack.org #> 6 NW ATLANTIC http://geo.gcoos.org/restore/ https://members.oceantrack.org #> id #> 1 #> 2 #> 3 #> 4 #> 5 #> 6 otn_list_stats() #> $project_count #> [1] 1601 #> #> $contributor_count #> [1] 2435 #> #> $inst_count #> [1] 467 #> #> $species_count #> [1] 457 #> #> $rcvr_count #> [1] 2984 otn_search_node("ACT") |> head() #> node collectioncode country longitude latitude shortname #> 1 ACT NJDEPMR USA -73.8185 38.0705 NJDEP Mullica River Array #> 2 ACT CTSEC6 USA -73.8150 38.0700 CT DEEP Sturgeon movements in CT, 2010-2017 #> 3 ACT CT001 USA -72.2500 41.6450 CT DEEP LIS array (Sec. 6 2018-2022) #> 4 ACT RIWFCC USA -73.8185 38.0705 Narragansett Bay Cable Corridor Monitoring #> 5 ACT CBASR USA -73.8150 38.0700 SERC Atlantic Stingray Study #> 6 ACT TNCCVOW USA -73.8185 38.0705 TNC / NEFSC CVOW tagging and array #> longname #> 1 NJDEP Mullica River Array #> 2 Identification of summering and foraging habitats of Atlantic Sturgeon in Connecticut waters and within Long Island Sound #> 3 CT DEEP array of VEMCO receivers in Long Island Sound and lower Connecticut River, 2018-2023. #> 4 Narragansett Bay Wind Farm Cable Corridor Monitoring #> 5 Atlantic Stingray Habitat Use and Migration #> 6 TNC / NEFSC CVOW tagging and array #> ocean website datacenter_infourl #> 1 NW ATLANTIC https://matos.asascience.com/ #> 2 NW ATLANTIC https://matos.asascience.com/ #> 3 NW ATLANTIC https://matos.asascience.com/ #> 4 NW ATLANTIC https://matos.asascience.com/ #> 5 NW ATLANTIC https://matos.asascience.com/ #> 6 NW ATLANTIC https://matos.asascience.com/ otn_search_code("tail") #> node collectioncode country longitude latitude shortname #> 1 ACT TAILWINDS USA -73.8185 38.0705 UMCES TailWinds #> longname ocean #> 1 TailWinds: Team for Assessing Impacts to Living resources from offshore WIND turbineS NW ATLANTIC #> website datacenter_infourl #> 1 https://tailwinds.umces.edu/ https://matos.asascience.com/ otn_search_contact("Mike O'Brien") #> node collectioncode country longitude latitude shortname #> 1 ACT CBBBMB USA -73.8150 38.0700 UMCES Chesapeake Backbone, Mid-Bay #> 2 ACT TAILWINDS USA -73.8185 38.0705 UMCES TailWinds #> 3 ACT MAMBON USA -73.8185 38.0705 Mid-Atlantic MBON #> 4 ACT NAVYKENN USA -69.7800 43.7750 Navy Kennebec ME Telemetry Array #> longname #> 1 'devel', batch_size = 5) #> name description #> 1 Copy of otn_tagging_metadata_2019.xls Manu tagging #> 2 NSBS_otn_metadata_tagging_2019 Reel Deal.xls #> 3 Tagging Metadata - Original #> 4 Tagging To check if any of your tags may have been missed please see the mystery tag list for your region or series. title: Call OTN API endpoints — .otn_api
text: Call OTN API endpoints

title: Call OTN API endpoints — .otn_api
Call OTN API endpoints — .otn_api
text: server Character. URL OTN-style Plone server. Defaults main OTN server https://members.oceantrack.org. endpoint Character. API endpoint call.

what: Return files associated with an OTN project — .otn_files
title: Return files associated with an OTN project — .otn_files
text: Return files associated OTN project

title: Return files associated with an OTN project — .otn_files
code: .otn_files(project, server = NULL, since = NULL, batch_size = NULL, type)

title: Return files associated with an OTN project — .otn_files
text: project Character. project code. server Character. URL OTN-style Plone server. Defaults main OTN server https://members.oceantrack.org. since Character. Filter files modified since date (YYYY-MM-DD format). batch_size Numeric. number results return. Defaults 25. type Character. Portion URL representing data type wish return.

title: Authenticate to an Ocean Tracking Network server — otn_login
Authenticate to an Ocean Tracking Network server — otn_login
text: server Character. URL OTN-style Plone server. Defaults main OTN server https://members.oceantrack.org. temporary Logical. Provide credentials current session ?

title: Authenticate to an Ocean Tracking Network server — otn_login
text: pop appear asking username password. everything works , token written "OTN_SESSION_TOKEN" system variable. username/password saved – done intentionally accidentally save credentials public script.

title: List OTN project files — otn_project_files
text: function lists file names, types, upload date, URLs OTN project files – basically everything see Data Metadata section project page. prompted log public project.

title: List OTN project files — otn_project_files
code: otn_project_files(project, server = NULL, since = NULL, batch_size = NULL)

title: List OTN project files — otn_project_files
text: project Character. project code. server Character. URL OTN-style Plone server. Defaults main OTN server https://members.oceantrack.org. since Character. Filter files modified since date (YYYY-MM-DD format). batch_size Numeric. number results return. Defaults 25.

what: Install your OTN username and password in your .Renviron File for repeated use — otn_set_credentials
title: Install your OTN username and password in your .Renviron File for repeated use — otn_set_credentials
text: code adapted tidycensus::census_api_key. Note saves credentials .Renviron, meaning anyone using computer can theoretically access MATOS username password . ... use carefully!

title: Install your OTN username and password in your .Renviron File for repeated use — otn_set_credentials
Install your OTN username and password in your .Renviron File for repeated use — otn_set_credentials
text: temporary Logical. Scrub credentials current session? overwrite Logical. Overwrite previously-stored MATOS credentials?

title: Install your OTN username and password in your .Renviron File for repeated use — otn_set_credentials

what: Set the OTN Plone instance with which you wish to interact. — otn_set_server
title: Set the OTN Plone instance with which you wish to interact. — otn_set_server
text: Set OTN Plone instance wish interact.

title: Set the OTN Plone instance with which you wish to interact. — otn_set_server
Set the OTN Plone instance with which you wish to interact. — otn_set_server
text: server Character. Lowercase network code desired server. One "otn", "npact", "act", "fact", "devel" (OTN development server).
